name: model-optimization
description: Model optimization: quantization, pruning, distillation, compilation. Covers PTQ, QAT, GPTQ, AWQ, HQQ, TensorRT, ONNX, TorchCompile, TVM, and inference optimization.
author: anwar

commands.name: opt:quantize
commands.prompt:
Create PTQ pipeline with:
- Methods: dynamic, static, weight-only, activation-aware
- Formats: INT8, INT4, FP8, MXFP4, NF4
- Tools: bitsandbytes, HQQ, AWQ, GPTQ, AutoAWQ, AutoGPTQ, QuaRot
- Calibration: minmax, entropy, percentile, MSE, KL divergence
- Granularity: per-tensor, per-channel, per-group, per-token
- Schemes: symmetric, asymmetric, affine
- LLM: AWQ (activation-aware), GPTQ (layerwise), HQQ (Hessian), QuaRot (rotation)
- Evaluation: perplexity, accuracy, latency, memory
- Export: safetensors, GGUF, ONNX, TensorRT
- Integration: HuggingFace, vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, llama.cpp

commands.name: opt:qat
commands.prompt:
Create QAT pipeline with:
- Fake quantization: per-tensor, per-channel, learned scale
- Straight-through estimator (STE), learned step size
- Methods: LSQ, PACT, AdaRound, BRECQ, QDrop
- Mixed precision: layer-wise, block-wise, automatic
- Schedule: gradual quantization, progressive quantization
- Distillation: teacher-student with quantized student
- Integration: PyTorch (torch.ao), TensorFlow (TF MOT), JAX
- Export: ONNX, TensorRT, TFLite, CoreML
- Validation: accuracy recovery, convergence monitoring

commands.name: opt:prune
commands.prompt:
Create pruning pipeline with:
- Structured: channel, filter, head, layer, block
- Unstructured: magnitude, movement, gradient, SNIP, GraSP
- Global vs layer-wise
- Sparsity: target, schedule (cubic, exponential, linear)
- Fine-tuning: recovery, knowledge distillation
- Tools: TorchPruner, NNCF, SparseML, TorchSparse
- Export: ONNX sparse, structured sparse tensors
- Hardware: NVIDIA sparse tensor cores (2:4)
- Composition: pruning + quantization + distillation
- Evaluation: accuracy vs sparsity, latency, memory

commands.name: opt:distill
commands.prompt:
Create distillation pipeline with:
- Types: response-based (logits), feature-based (intermediate), relation-based
- Loss: KL divergence, MSE, cosine, PKT, FSP, Attention transfer
- Teacher: ensemble, single, progressive
- Student: smaller architecture, quantized, pruned
- Data: original, augmented, synthetic (generative)
- Temperature: scheduling, adaptive
- Multi-teacher: ensemble, specialized teachers
- Self-distillation: born-again, online, snapshot
- Tools: Distiller, TorchDistill, custom
- Evaluation: accuracy gap, calibration, robustness

commands.name: opt:compile
commands.prompt:
Create compilation pipeline with:
- TorchCompile: inductor, aot_autograd, cudagraphs, dynamic shapes
- TensorRT: ONNX parser, builder, calibration, plugins
- ONNX Runtime: graph optimization, execution providers
- TVM: Relay, AutoScheduler, AutoTensorize, meta-schedule
- OpenVINO: model optimizer, runtime, NPU/GPU/CPU
- XLA: PJRT, SPMD, GSPMD
- MPS/Metal: CoreML, MPSGraph
- Triton: custom kernels, autotuning
- Batching: static, dynamic, ragged
- Profiler: NSight, torch.profiler, TensorRT Profiler
- Deployment: Triton, TorchServe, BentoML, vLLM

commands.name: opt:inference
commands.prompt:
Create inference optimization with:
- Engines: vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, TGI, llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime
- Batching: continuous, paged attention, chunked prefill
- Scheduling: FCFS, priority, speculation-aware
- Speculative decoding: draft model, Medusa, EAGLE, Lookahead, Hydra
- Quantization: AWQ, GPTQ, FP8, INT4, INT8
- KV Cache: quantization, offloading, paging, compression
- Parallelism: tensor, pipeline, data, expert
- Prefill-decode disaggregation
- Metrics: TTFT, TBT, throughput, latency percentiles
- Autoscaling: request-based, metric-based, predictive

agents.name: optimization-engineer
agents.prompt: You are a model optimization engineer. Expert in: quantization (PTQ/QAT/GPTQ/AWQ/HQQ), pruning (structured/unstructured), distillation (logit/feature/relation), compilation (TorchCompile/TensorRT/TVM/OpenVINO), inference (vLLM/SGLang/TensorRT-LLM). Focus on: accuracy preservation, latency reduction, throughput maximization, memory efficiency, hardware utilization. Write production optimization pipelines with benchmarks.
